title: Registry audit
text: audit_everything() walks replication registry attempts every table figure listed engine (R Stata exist). meant health check: failures expected studies, data, dependencies change time. Key behaviour: patience (default 20 seconds) — table figure halted limit; audit continues next object. Failures stop run — results collected data frame. Report fields — study, object id, engine, success, elapsed seconds, timed-flag, short error snippet failure. can restrict specific DOIs render Quarto report registry repository:
code: library(replicateEverything)  # Point at a local monorepo checkout (optional) options(   replicateEverything.registry_root = "/path/to/replicate_everything/registry",   replicateEverything.study_folders_root = "/path/to/replicate_everything",   replicateEverything.use_sibling_packages = TRUE )  audit <- audit_everything(patience = 20) print(audit) audit <- audit_everything(patience = 30, dois = "10.1257/aer.91.5.1369")  # Quarto report lives in the registry repo (sibling registry/ in a monorepo) quarto::quarto_render(audit_everything_qmd(), execute_params = list(patience = 20))

title: Registry audit
text: table built saved audit snapshot shipped package (inst/vignette-data/audit_latest.rds). Set REPLICATE_AUDIT_LIVE=true (point local monorepo) intend refresh snapshot.
code: audit <- if (run_live) {   tryCatch({     monorepo <- Sys.getenv("REPLICATE_MONOREPO", unset = "")     if (!nzchar(monorepo)) {       parent <- normalizePath(         file.path(find.package("replicateEverything"), "..", ".."),         mustWork = FALSE       )       if (file.exists(file.path(parent, "registry", "index.csv"))) {         monorepo <- parent       }     }     if (nzchar(monorepo)) {       options(         replicateEverything.registry_root = file.path(monorepo, "registry"),         replicateEverything.study_folders_root = monorepo,         replicateEverything.use_sibling_packages = TRUE       )     }     audit_everything(patience = 20, verbose = FALSE)   }, error = function(e) {     message("Live audit skipped: ", conditionMessage(e))     NULL   }) } else {   NULL }  if (is.null(audit) && nzchar(audit_rds) && file.exists(audit_rds)) {   audit <- readRDS(audit_rds) } if (is.null(audit)) {   stop("No audit results available.") } sm <- audit$summary results <- audit$results

code: fails <- results[!results$success, , drop = FALSE] if (nrow(fails) == 0) {   cat("All recorded runs succeeded.\n") } else {   fails$seconds <- ifelse(is.na(fails$seconds), NA, round(fails$seconds, 2))   fails$status <- ifelse(fails$timed_out, "Timed out", "Failed")   show <- fails[, c(     "title", "object_label", "object", "engine",     "status", "seconds", "error_snippet"   )]   names(show) <- c(     "Study", "Object", "ID", "Engine", "Status", "Seconds", "Error"   )   knitr::kable(show, row.names = FALSE) } #> All recorded runs succeeded.

title: Registry audit
text: Common reasons run fails times : Missing study package folder — install study repo locally set replicateEverything.study_folders_root monorepo root. Stata installed — Stata-backed entries fail Stata found; see Stata replications vignette. Network / data — folder-backed studies may need data files downloaded first run. Patience low — slow tables may need higher patience value without indicating true failure. Re-run locally refresh package vignette snapshot:
code: Sys.setenv(REPLICATE_AUDIT_LIVE = "true") options(   replicateEverything.registry_root = "/path/to/replicate_everything/registry",   replicateEverything.study_folders_root = "/path/to/replicate_everything",   replicateEverything.use_sibling_packages = TRUE ) audit <- audit_everything(patience = 20) saveRDS(audit, "inst/vignette-data/audit_latest.rds")  # Full HTML report: quarto render audit_everything.qmd (in the registry repo)

what: Find a paper in the registry
title: Meet the functions
text: Start index. load_index() returns registry index.csv data frame (DOI, title, journal, year, authors, study repo). Example output: search_papers() keyword-searches titles authors. Example output: one paper, list_replications() lists every registered table figure, including engine (r / stata) declared. Example output (abbreviated): list_replication_groups() returns one entry per logical group. R Stata exist table, default R.
code: idx <- load_index() head(idx[, c("doi", "title", "year")]) #>                              doi                                    title year #> 1 10.1177/00491241211036161   Bounding Causes of Effects with Randomized... 2022 #> 2 10.1017/S0003055403000534   Ethnicity, Insurgency, and Civil War        2003 search_papers("insurgency") #>                              doi                                  title #> 1 10.1017/S0003055403000534 Ethnicity, Insurgency, and Civil War list_replications("10.1177/00491241211036161") #> [[1]] #> $id #> [1] "fig_1" #> $type #> [1] "figure" list_replication_groups("10.1257/aer.91.5.1369")

title: Meet the functions
text: run_replication() main entry point. Pass DOI (registry handle) replication id ("fig_1", "tab_1", ). default get analysis object — ggplot, model, data.frame. title: Stata replications
text: replicateEverything R package. orchestrates replications R: loading registry metadata, fetching study folders packages, running code. Stata-backed entries first-class: package calls Stata batch mode, captures logs images, can format display R Shiny. can also register bilingual studies paired R Stata entries (group, different engine). Acemoglu et al. study registry example: table tab_N (R) tab_N_stata (Stata).

what: How Stata replications work in R
title: Stata replications
text: replication.yml marks entry engine: stata (.file path). run_replication() detects engine runs Stata batch mode language = "stata" (Stata engine). R locates Stata via find_stata_executable(), writes small runner, invokes Stata batch mode (StataMP-64.exe /e ... Windows). Output returned R object class stata_replication_result — list (minimum) output_path (log image), study_root, replication_id. entry defines format: code/format_stata.R, R reads Stata log builds HTML table Shiny / format = TRUE. returned object Stata dataset inside R; format = FALSE get handle Stata output disk (usually .log file). Use format = TRUE HTML table format: step registered.

title: Stata replications
text: returns NULL, R stop clear error run Stata replication: Replication tab_1_stata requires Stata. Install Stata set options(replicateEverything.stata_executable = …). Point R installation: find_stata_executable() checks, order: getOption("replicateEverything.stata_executable") PATH (stata-mp, stata-se, …) Common install locations (Windows Program Files, /Applications/Stata/…, Linux /usr/local/stata*)

title: replicateEverything
text: Tools discover, run, contribute computational replications empirical research papers. replicateEverything connects public replication registry, retrieves replication materials (metadata, processed data, analysis code), reproduces figures tables published studies standardized workflow. package also bundles Shiny demo app browsing studies running replications interactively — try live demo.

title: replicateEverything
text: Discovery — search registry, look papers DOI, inspect available replications One-line replication — run single figure table, reproduce entire paper run_replication(doi, "everything") Registry-backed materials — fetch data code GitHub without manual downloads Folder-backed studies — dedicated study repositories code/, data/, artifacts/ Package-backed studies — standalone R packages linked lightweight registry stubs Artifacts — load, validate, save precomputed outputs (PNG, HTML, RDS) fast display Display pipeline — optional format_* steps turn analysis objects HTML tables ggplot figures Shiny demo — live app; run_shiny_app() locally; save_local_shiny() deploy Shiny Server Contributor tooling — validate register folder- package-backed studies (prepare_folder_paper(), check_folder_replication(), check_package_replication()) Bundled AI skills — markdown workflow guides assistants (ai_skills(), ai_skill())

title: replicateEverything 0.5.0
text: Live Run Shiny never install dependencies host default. probe (R requireNamespace, Python importlib, Stata stata_deps_probe). Maintainer builds call build_study_artifacts(install_deps = TRUE), sets options(replicateEverything.install_dependencies = TRUE) install_stata_deps = TRUE session . Study runners must call install_stata_deps.init_study_paths.table scripts.

title: replicateEverything 0.5.0
text: Stata dependency checks study-declared via stata_deps_probe: (check-.study repo) stata_packages: (generic -probe). package longer hardcodes ftools/reghdfe/estout. Live Run probes ; install_stata_deps.runs options(replicateEverything.install_stata_deps = TRUE) (e.g. build_study_artifacts(install_deps = TRUE)). Stata dependency probe study install_stata_deps.load tests use help reghdfe instead bare reghdfe applicable (study probe script). Invoking reghdfe data returns r(301) even installed. satisfied (typical dev machine), install script skipped Shiny shows “Stata dependencies OK — skipped install”. Install runs probe fails. Progress lines reported via replicate_progress() / options(replicateEverything.progress) Shiny’s “Working:” banner. Stata batch runs honour timeout suggested processx package installed; overdue runs killed Shiny can recover instead freezing indefinitely. Tune options(replicateEverything.stata_timeout), stata_deps_probe_timeout (default 120s), stata_deps_install_timeout (default 600s). Set options(replicateEverything.install_stata_deps = FALSE) skip study Stata installs entirely. Shiny artifact loading longer passes install_deps = TRUE (live Run installs dependencies). Study cache downloads unzip filesystem cache directory (system /tmp), moving extracted repo cache atomic Linux servers /tmp Samba/NFS cache different mounts. cross-device file.rename fallback also uses single recursive copy suppresses EXDEV warning. Study Stata dependency scripts (install_stata_deps.etc.) now run per study per session instead every prep step table, repeated live runs longer re-trigger slow SSC reinstall/recompile. missing-dependency retry still forces re-run. Set options(replicateEverything.install_stata_deps = FALSE) skip study dependency installation entirely manage Stata packages . Cached GitHub study checkouts now refresh remote commit changes. materialize_folder_study_from_github() records downloaded commit SHA compares current remote SHA (via GitHub API); stale cache (e.g. one built new data files committed) re-downloaded automatically. remote SHA determined (offline rate-limited) existing cache kept. fixes live Stata/Python runs failing “file found” data exists repo missing --date server cache. remote check -demand (study actually run/fetched GitHub) skipped short, per-session window study confirmed fresh, repeated resolutions within one run make one API call per study; tune options(replicateEverything.study_cache_ttl = <seconds>) (default 300; 0 always check). Python replications now run resolved study folder (local sibling materialized GitHub clone) instead falling back R working directory. Shiny server fixes FileNotFoundError REPLICATE_STUDY_ROOT pointed app directory (e.g. ShinyApps/replicate/data/raw/...) rather study repo clone. Python process now also runs working directory set study root, matching Stata. Python dependency probing uses importlib.util.find_spec per package, prefers Windows py -0p launcher installs Store stubs, skips WindowsApps aliases PATH. Compatibility UI shows full Python path probed. ai_skills() longer lists README.md skill; inst/ai/skills/ now bundles folder_replication APSR_to_replicateEverything.

text: Removed deprecated replicate_paper() create_replication_template(). Use run_replication(doi, "everything") folder/package replication checklists instead. Pre-built vignette HTML shipped inst/doc/ installs skip vignette builds still include articles.

title: replicateEverything 0.4.0
text: Slim export surface (~14 functions): discovery, run, Shiny, contribute, audit helpers . run_replication(doi, = "everything") replaces replicate_paper() full-paper runs. Registry index.csv includes handle column; search_papers() run functions accept handles (e.g. "bounding-causes"). validate_replication() internal helpers longer exported. New vignette: Meet functions.

title: replicateEverything 0.3.0
text: build_study_artifacts() — run replications write artifacts/ + manifest.json study repo. check_folder_replication() — pre-merge checklist (layout, yaml, code/data paths, artifacts, tests). prepare_folder_paper() — build artifacts, validate, write registry/replication.yml + registry/index.csv study repo. sync_folder_paper() — copy prepared stub files registry checkout. add_folder_paper() — validate register folder-backed study stub registry. audit_everything() — registry-wide audit (Quarto report: audit_everything.qmd registry repo). Vignettes: Registry audit Stata replications. Improved artifact error hints folder-backed studies.

title: replicateEverything 0.2.0
text: Connect public replication registry discover run computational replications DOI. Run single figure table run_replication() / render_replication(), reproduce entire paper replicate_paper(). Load, validate, save precomputed artifacts (load_artifact(), save_artifact(), validate_artifact()). Optional display pipeline via registered format_* functions (format_for_display(), render_for_display()). Package-backed replications: install call standalone study packages registry local monorepo. Contributor tooling: create_replication_template() scaffolds new replication folder replication.yml, data, code stubs. Registry search metadata helpers: search_papers(), load_index(), get_doi_metadata(), list_replications(), get_code(). Local registry development via options(replicateEverything.registry_root = ...) options(replicateEverything.index = ...).
